story.heading: Gannett and USA TODAY Network Outline AI Ethics and Operational Policies
story.summary: The USA TODAY Network and Gannett have established comprehensive guidelines for the ethical use of artificial intelligence, alongside updated accessibility features and subscription terms for their digital and print platforms.

segments.heading: Ethical Framework for Artificial Intelligence
segments.text: Gannett and the USA TODAY Network have implemented strict ethical guidelines to govern the use of AI-generated content. Journalists are required to consult with editors and the company's AI Council before utilizing AI tools. The policy emphasizes transparency and accountability, specifically prohibiting the use of AI-generated photo-realistic images in news coverage to maintain journalistic integrity.

citations.quote: Gannett does not use AI-generated photo-realistic images in our news coverage.
Journalists must discuss the purpose and production of any AI-generated content with their editor and Gannett’s AI Council before using it.

segments.heading: Digital Accessibility and User Support
segments.text: The network is enhancing its digital platforms to ensure accessibility for all users. This includes support for screen readers, zoom functionality, and color contrast adjustments on both Windows and OS X systems. Features such as 'Narrator' for Windows and 'Voiceover' for Mac are highlighted as key tools for navigating the desktop and mobile sites.

citations.quote: The USA TODAY NETWORK is committed to making its desktop site, mobile website, and mobile applications accessible for all viewers.

segments.heading: Subscription Terms and Delivery Protocols
segments.text: Subscription policies for USA TODAY include provisions for non-print days, during which subscribers receive an eNewspaper replica. The terms also detail 'EZ Pay' options to avoid paper statement fees and explain how USPS mail delivery affects print arrival during federal holidays. Digital access remains constant even if print delivery is suspended for vacations.

citations.quote: An eNewspaper edition will be provided on non-print days.
A printed subscription invoice includes a $5.00 paper statement fee. To avoid this fee, subscribers can choose to set up EZ Pay.

story.summary: Digital news subscriptions offer users unlimited access to content across multiple devices alongside exclusive member perks.

segments.heading: Access and Content Features
segments.text: Subscribers to digital news platforms typically receive unrestricted access to articles on various devices. Additionally, these services often provide exclusive content and specialized newsletters tailored for members.

citations.quote: Access unlimited news on all your devices, receive subscriber-only newsletters, exclusive content, and tons of perks.

story.heading: 2026 Winter Olympics: Medal Standings and Key Results from February 18-21
story.summary: Norway leads the overall medal count at the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics as the games enter their final days, highlighted by record-breaking performances in cross-country skiing and significant podium finishes in curling and speed skating.

segments.heading: Current Medal Standings
segments.text: As of mid-February, Norway maintains a significant lead in the overall medal tally with 33 total medals, including 15 gold. Italy follows in second place with 26 medals, while the United States holds third with 24. Other top-performing nations include Japan, Germany, Austria, and France. The competition features athletes from over 90 countries competing in 116 events.

citations.quote: Norway: 33 Total (15 Gold, 8 Silver, 10 Bronze)
Athletes from more than 90 countries will compete for Winter Olympic medals in 116 events over 16 days

segments.heading: Record-Breaking Performances
segments.text: Norwegian cross-country skier Johannes Høsflot Klæbo achieved a historic milestone by winning his sixth gold medal of these Games in the 50-kilometer mass start. This marks the most gold medals won by any athlete in a single Winter Olympics, surpassing the previous record held by American Eric Heiden. Meanwhile, American speedskater Jordan Stolz concluded his competition with two golds and a silver, setting Olympic records in the 500-meter and 1,000-meter events.

citations.quote: The 29-year-old Norwegian’s six gold medals in these Games are the most ever won in a single Olympics.
two golds and a silver, I’m pretty happy with that.

segments.heading: Team Event Results
segments.text: In men's curling, Canada secured the gold medal with a 9-6 victory over Great Britain, marking Canada's first title in the event since 2014. Great Britain received the silver medal for the second consecutive Games. In men's ice hockey, Finland earned the bronze medal after defeating Slovakia 6-1. The men's gold medal hockey game between the USA and Canada is scheduled for Sunday morning.

citations.quote: On Saturday, he took out Great Britain 9-6 in a tense and strategic battle to win his second Olympic gold medal and a fourth men’s curling title for Canada
Finland put the disappointment of Friday’s narrow loss to Canada in the rearview mirror and blitzed Slovakia 6-1 to claim the bronze medal in men’s hockey.

segments.heading: Upcoming Schedule
segments.text: The final days of competition include several high-profile medal events. Scheduled for February 19 through February 21 are the women's ice hockey final, the women's figure skating free skate, and various finals in freestyle skiing, speed skating, and biathlon. The games will also feature the men's and women's curling medal matches and the conclusion of the bobsled events.

citations.quote: ICE HOCKEY: Women's Final; FIGURE SKATING: Women's Free Skate

story.heading: Pakistan Conducts Airstrikes Against Militant Targets in Afghanistan
story.summary: Pakistan has launched a series of airstrikes against militant camps in Afghanistan following a surge in cross-border attacks, leading to reports of civilian casualties and a sharp diplomatic rebuke from Kabul.

segments.heading: Military Operations and Targeted Groups
segments.text: Pakistan's military conducted what it described as 'intelligence-based, selective operations' against seven militant camps in Afghanistan. The strikes targeted the Tehrik-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP), its affiliates, and a group associated with the Islamic State. Islamabad stated the operations were a response to recent deadly attacks on Pakistani soil, including a suicide bombing at a mosque in Islamabad and an attack in the Bajaur district that killed 11 soldiers.

citations.quote: Pakistan’s information ministry said its military conducted ‘intelligence-based, selective operations’ against seven camps belonging to militants it blames for a recent series of deadly attacks on its soil.
The latest development came days after a suicide bomber, backed by gunmen, rammed an explosives-laden vehicle into the wall of a security post in Bajaur district... killing 11 soldiers and a child.

segments.heading: Casualties and Impact in Afghanistan
segments.text: The Afghan Ministry of Defense reported that the strikes occurred in the eastern provinces of Nangarhar and Paktika, hitting civilian homes and a religious seminary. Afghan officials stated that women and children were among the victims, with reports indicating at least 18 people were killed. Rescue efforts continued on Sunday as bodies were recovered from the debris.

citations.quote: Women and children were among the 18 people killed, Sayed Tayeb Hamad, a senior police official in Nangarhar, said on Afghan state television.
In a post on X, Mujahid said the strikes targeted civilians in Nangarhar and Paktika provinces, killing and wounding dozens of people, including women and children.

segments.heading: Diplomatic Tensions and Sovereignty
segments.text: The Afghan government condemned the strikes as a 'blatant violation' of national sovereignty and international law. Conversely, Pakistan's information minister, Attaullah Tarar, claimed the country possesses 'conclusive evidence' that recent attacks were directed by leadership based in Afghanistan. Pakistan has urged the international community to pressure the Taliban to prevent Afghan soil from being used for cross-border militancy, a charge Kabul continues to deny.

citations.quote: Afghanistan’s Ministry of Defense confirmed the strikes in a statement, calling them a ‘blatant violation of Afghanistan’s national sovereignty’.
Tarar said Pakistan had ‘conclusive evidence’ that the recent attacks... were carried out by militants acting at the ‘behest of their Afghanistan-based leadership and handlers’.

story.heading: Northeast Braces for Significant Nor'easter and Blizzard Conditions
story.summary: A major winter storm is set to impact the U.S. Northeast starting Sunday, prompting blizzard warnings for major metropolitan areas and states of emergency across several regions.

segments.heading: Storm Forecast and Severity
segments.text: Meteorologists expect the storm to rapidly intensify into a 'bomb cyclone' as it moves off the Eastern Seaboard. Blizzard warnings have been issued for New York City, Philadelphia, and coastal New England, marking the first such warning for New York City since 2017. Snowfall totals are projected to reach between 12 and 20 inches in the hardest-hit areas, accompanied by wind gusts exceeding 55 mph.

citations.quote: The storm will rapidly intensify into a bomb cyclone off the Eastern Seaboard beginning on Sunday
The National Weather Service increased its assessment of the severity of the storm, saying it anticipated 1ft to 2 ft of snow for New York City

segments.heading: Emergency Preparations and Government Response
segments.text: Governors in New Jersey and New York have declared states of emergency, while local officials in Philadelphia and Washington, D.C., have activated snow response teams. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani warned that conditions may be more hazardous than previous storms this season, leading the city to open over 40 warming centers and increase outreach for the unhoused population.

citations.quote: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill declared a state of emergency to start at noon Sunday, as forecasts call for significant snowfall across the region.
efforts to get unhoused New Yorkers off the streets and into shelters would begin Saturday afternoon.

segments.heading: Travel and Infrastructure Impacts
segments.text: Significant travel disruptions are anticipated, with approximately 2,000 flights already canceled ahead of the storm's peak. Officials have warned that travel may become impossible by Sunday night and Monday morning. Beyond transportation, the combination of heavy, wet snow and high winds is expected to cause power outages and downed tree limbs across the region.

citations.quote: Around 2,000 Sunday flights have been canceled, according to FlightAware . The disruptions are expected to heavily impact New York City-area airports
Scattered downed tree limbs and power outages possible due to snow load and strong winds.

The forecast has been fraught with uncertainty for days, but has finally homed…

story.heading: Recovery Operations Conclude Following Deadly Sierra Nevada Avalanche
story.summary: Authorities have recovered the bodies of nine individuals killed in a Tuesday avalanche near Lake Tahoe, marking the deadliest such event in the United States in 45 years.

segments.heading: Incident Overview and Casualties
segments.text: On Tuesday, February 17, 2026, an avalanche approximately the size of a football field struck a group of backcountry skiers in the Tahoe National Forest. The disaster resulted in nine fatalities, including six women from a close-knit group of friends and three professional guides from Blackbird Mountain Guides. Six other members of the party survived the slide. The victims have been identified as Carrie Atkin, Liz Clabaugh, Danielle Keatley, Kate Morse, Caroline Sekar, Kate Vitt, and guides Andrew Alissandratos, Nicole Choo, and Michael Henry.

citations.quote: Six of the close friends and three guides are among the nine people killed in the avalanche near California’s Lake Tahoe – the nation’s deadliest in 45 years.
The sheriff named for the first time the three guides from Blackbird Mountain Company who died: Andrew Alissandratos, 34, Nicole Choo, 42, and Michael Henry, 30.

segments.heading: Search and Recovery Efforts
segments.text: The recovery operation spanned five days, hampered by severe winter weather and heavy snowfall. While six survivors were rescued shortly after the incident, the remains of the deceased were recovered in stages, with the final five bodies located on Saturday morning. The operation involved a coordinated effort between the Nevada County Sheriff’s Office, the California Highway Patrol, the National Guard, and dozens of volunteers. Air support, including a Black Hawk helicopter, was utilized once weather conditions permitted.

citations.quote: Saturday’s recovery of the bodies marked the end of a five-day effort to locate nine missing skiers after one of the deadliest avalanches in recent history.
The first call for help was a silent text message from an emergency beacon, mobilizing a small army of rescuers dispatched from different directions.

segments.heading: Investigation and Access Restrictions
segments.text: The Nevada County Sheriff’s Office is currently investigating the circumstances of the avalanche, including whether criminal negligence played a role. The group was on a professionally guided tour and was returning from the Frog Lake Backcountry Huts when the slide occurred during a major winter storm. As a result of the ongoing investigation and safety concerns, the Tahoe National Forest has closed the affected terrain to the public until mid-March.

citations.quote: The sheriff’s office said it is investigating whether criminal negligence contributed to the incident.
The terrain will be off-limits to visitors until mid-March, said Chris Feutrier, forest supervisor for the Tahoe national forest.

story.heading: Trump Increases Global Tariffs to 15% Following Supreme Court Ruling
story.summary: President Donald Trump has announced a 15% global tariff on imports from all countries, a move that follows a Supreme Court decision striking down his previous use of emergency powers to impose trade duties.

segments.heading: New Tariff Announcement and Legal Context
segments.text: On Saturday, President Trump announced via Truth Social that he would increase global tariffs to 15%, up from a 10% rate he had proposed just a day earlier. This escalation followed a 6-3 Supreme Court ruling on Friday, which determined that the president had exceeded his authority by using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) to impose broad trade duties. In response, Trump invoked Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974, a law allowing for temporary 15% duties for up to 150 days without immediate congressional approval.

citations.quote: The court on Friday ruled that Trump exceeded his authority with the way he imposed tariffs on trading partners using the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA)
The law according to section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 – which has never been used – allows the president to impose a levy of up to 15% for 150 days

segments.heading: Implementation and Economic Impact
segments.text: While Trump stated the new 15% rate is effective "immediately," official documentation regarding the exact timing remains unclear. Previous guidance suggested a 10% rate would take effect on Tuesday, February 24. Economists note that while some countries previously facing much higher "reciprocal" duties might see a relative decrease in rates, others like the United Kingdom and Australia will face higher costs. Domestically, while some retailers and automakers may benefit from the removal of higher specific duties, experts warn that US consumers and businesses have historically borne the majority of tariff costs.

citations.quote: The 15% duties would make this year’s effective tariff rate 6%, according to Erica York, vice president of federal tax policy at the Tax Foundation.
studies show that the vast majority of that sum – 90% – has been paid by US businesses and consumers.

segments.heading: International and Domestic Reactions
segments.text: The announcement has drawn sharp criticism from international leaders and domestic opposition. Australian officials described the tariffs as "unjustified" and "regrettable," signaling they would seek exemptions. German Chancellor Friedrich Merz warned that constant tariff uncertainty acts as "poison" for the global economy, while French President Emmanuel Macron emphasized the importance of the rule of law and judicial counterweights. Within the US, business associations have already begun seeking refunds for duties previously collected under the now-overturned IEEPA framework.

citations.quote: The shadow defence minister, James Paterson, told Sky News on Sunday that Trump’s announcement was ‘regrettable and unfortunate’.
German chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Saturday... ‘The biggest poison for the economies of Europe and the US is this constant uncertainty about tariffs.’

articles.title: Trump announces global tariffs will increase to 15% from 10% ‘effective immediately’
articles.excerpt:
President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he will increase the global tariffs he imposed a day earlier to 15% from 10% following an adverse ruling at the Supreme Court.

The court on Friday ruled t…

The US president’s move came less than 24 hours…

contradiction_reports.correct_claim: A White House fact sheet issued on Friday regarding the original 10% tariffs said the levies would go into effect at 12.01am ET on Tuesday, 24 February.

trump-announces-global-tariffs-will-incr: A White House fact sheet issued on Friday regarding the original 10% tariffs said the levies would go into effect at 12.01am ET on Tuesday, 24 February.
australia-will-examine-all-options-to-av: A White House fact sheet issued on Friday regarding the original 10% tariffs said the levies would go into effect at 12.01am ET on Tuesday, 24 February.

story.heading: Ukraine Condemns Energy Supply Threats from Hungary and Slovakia
story.summary: Ukraine's Foreign Ministry has denounced threats from Hungary and Slovakia to halt electricity exports as "blackmail," following a dispute over the suspension of Russian oil flows through the Druzhba pipeline.

segments.heading: The Energy Dispute and Pipeline Outage
segments.text: Shipments of Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia via the Druzhba pipeline have been suspended since January 27. Kyiv attributes the outage to a Russian drone strike that damaged pipeline equipment in Western Ukraine. However, Hungary and Slovakia have accused Ukraine of intentionally delaying repairs and the resumption of oil transit. In response, Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico and Hungarian leader Viktor Orban have threatened to cut off emergency electricity supplies to Ukraine, which currently relies on these neighbors for approximately half of its European electricity imports.

citations.quote: Slovakia and Hungary say Ukraine is to blame for the prolonged outage.
Hungary and Slovakia both accuse Ukraine of delaying the restart, without evidence.

segments.heading: Diplomatic Tensions and Accusations
segments.text: The Ukrainian Foreign Ministry issued a statement rejecting what it termed "ultimatums and blackmail," arguing that such pressure should be directed at the Kremlin rather than Kyiv. The dispute highlights a growing rift between Ukraine and the two EU and NATO members, whose leaders have maintained closer ties with Moscow than many of their European counterparts. Slovak Prime Minister Fico has further escalated the rhetoric by accusing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of acting maliciously toward Slovakia.

citations.quote: Ukraine rejects and condemns the ultimatums and blackmail by the governments of Hungary and the Slovak Republic.
The Slovak leader, Robert Fico, has accused Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelenskyy of acting ‘maliciously’ towards his country.

segments.heading: Proposed Solutions and Regional Security
segments.text: Ukraine has proposed alternative transit routes to maintain oil flows to Europe during the repair period, including the use of its domestic oil transportation system or maritime routes via the Odesa-Brody pipeline. Kyiv has characterized the threats to its electricity supply as irresponsible actions that jeopardize the energy security of the entire region, especially as Russia continues to target Ukraine's power grid with missile and drone strikes.

citations.quote: Ukraine has proposed alternative transit routes to ship oil to Europe while emergency pipeline repair works are under way.

segments.heading: Ongoing Military Developments
segments.text: As the conflict nears its fourth anniversary, military activity remains intense. Explosions were reported in Kyiv and Lviv, with the latter resulting in the death of a policewoman and injuries to 15 others. Meanwhile, Ukraine confirmed strikes against a missile factory in Russia's Udmurt Republic using domestically produced cruise missiles, and a strike on a gas plant in Samara.

citations.quote: Ukraine’s General Staff confirmed it used Ukrainian-made FP-5 ‘Flamingo’ cruise missiles against the Votkinsk plant.

“Ultimatums should ⁠be sent to the Kremlin, and certainly not to Kyiv.”

Hungary, Slovakia are key for Ukraine’s electricity imports

Between them, Hungary and Slovakia have been providing around half ‌of European emergency electricity exports to Ukraine, which Kyiv increasingly relies on ​as Russian attacks have damaged its grid.
contradiction_reports.correct_claim:
“Ultimatums should be sent to the Kremlin, and certainly not to Kyiv.”

Slovakia and Hungary are the only two EU countries that still rely on significant amounts of Russian oil shipped via the Soviet-era Druzhba pipeline over Ukraine.

story.heading: Willie Colón, Influential Salsa Trombonist and Composer, Dies at 75
story.summary: American salsa legend Willie Colón, a pioneering trombonist, vocalist, and composer who helped define the genre and sold over 30 million albums, has died at the age of 75.

segments.heading: Death and Family Statement
segments.text: Willie Colón passed away peacefully on Saturday morning at the age of 75, surrounded by his family. While the specific cause of death was not disclosed, his family released a statement on his Facebook page expressing their grief and celebrating his musical legacy. His longtime manager, Pietro Carlos, noted that Colón's work expanded and politicized the salsa genre.

citations.quote: He passed away peacefully this morning surrounded by his beloved family.
The cause of death was not disclosed.

segments.heading: Musical Career and Style
segments.text: Born in the Bronx to Puerto Rican parents, Colón signed with Fania Records at age 15 and released his debut album, 'El Malo', in 1967. His music was characterized by a fusion of jazz, rock, and salsa, incorporating rhythms from Cuba, Puerto Rico, Brazil, and Africa. Throughout his career, he recorded dozens of albums, including 'La Gran Fuga' and 'El Juicio'. His 1978 collaboration with Rubén Blades, 'Siembra', remains the highest-selling salsa album of all time.

citations.quote: Colón’s music combined elements of jazz, rock and salsa, incorporating the rhythms of traditional music from Cuba, Puerto Rico, Brazil, and Africa
His 1978 album Siembra, recorded with Rubén Blades, includes the hit Pedro Navaja and remains the biggest-selling salsa album of all time.

segments.heading: Awards and Social Activism
segments.text: Colón received numerous accolades, including a lifetime achievement award from the Latin Academy of Recording Arts and Sciences in 2004 and inductions into the International Latin Music Hall of Fame and the Latin Songwriters Hall of Fame. Beyond music, he was a dedicated social activist, serving with the Latino Commission on AIDS, the United Nations Immigrant Foundation, and the Congressional Hispanic Caucus Institute. His work often explored the cultural identity and challenges of Puerto Ricans living in the United States.

citations.quote: A longtime social activist, Colón was a member of the Latino Commission on AIDS and the United Nations Immigrant Foundation
Billboard magazine named the musician as one of the 30 most influential Latin artists of all time in 2015.
